\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5"Slower and Steadier"\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

Michael Eisenband, the global co-leader of corporate finance and restructuring at FTI Consulting Inc. ($FCN), recently pondered the state of the industry as it moves deeper into the second half of \xe2\x80\x9824. The first half? Could be worse, he says. \xe2\x80\x9cIt was a busy first-half, despite the occasional snide remark from Petition that restructuring advisors have had ample time to shave a couple of strokes off their golf handicaps in recent months.\xe2\x80\x9d* But it could also be a lot better: there were 85 large \xe2\x80\x93\xe2\x80\x94 >$50mm, LOL, \xe2\x80\x9clarge\xe2\x80\x9d** \xe2\x80\x94 chapter 11 filings in the first half, a 17% decrease from 102 in the first half of last year (but 23 filings over >$1b debt cases versus 20 in 1H23), and advisor mandates fell 19% in the first half of \xe2\x80\x9824 to 261 from 323. To be sure, 1H24 is up against a tough comp but clearly we have at least some reason to be snarking about golf.***

The not-as-bad-as-it-seems commentary may be cold comfort for restructuring pros, even veterans familiar with the cycle of long stretches of boredom punctuated by furious bursts of activity. \xe2\x80\x9cThat paradigm may be broken,\xe2\x80\x9d he writes. \xe2\x80\x9cGiven the explosive growth of leveraged credit markets over the last decade, the ascendance of private credit, the extreme lengths that PE sponsors will resort to in their efforts to protect teetering investments, and the reluctance of traditional lenders to foreclose on failing credits, the days of intense but short-lived default cycles might be over, barring a cataclysmic event.\xe2\x80\x9d There may be a \xe2\x80\x9cslower and steadier\xe2\x80\x9d paradigm afoot now, he says.

We\xe2\x80\x99ve seen a lot of what he points to recently, yeah? There\xe2\x80\x99s the relentless increase in distressed exchanges and LMEs. According to (more recent) data from S&P Global Inc., there have been forty-five (45) distressed exchanges so far this year, an 18% increase over the same period last year and the highest level since 2009. Some two-thirds of July\xe2\x80\x99s defaults were distressed exchanges:

The monthly defaulted debt tally fell to $6.4b in July, its second-lowest level this year, and 21% below June\xe2\x80\x99s $8.1b, even as the number of defaulters is touching its five-year average:

So, what does \xe2\x80\x9cslower and steadier\xe2\x80\x9d mean for those of us who aren\xe2\x80\x99t architecting LME deals? Mr. Eisenband states there are signs the economy is slowing,**** notes that rates are unlikely to return to the QE-era zero bound any time soon, and highlights that corporate America has plenty of zombies on offer. \xe2\x80\x9cIt is still a target-rich environment of vulnerable companies out there \xe2\x80\x94 but it won\xe2\x80\x99t happen as quickly or intensely as many of us would prefer. Ultimately, that may be a good thing,\xe2\x80\x9d he writes. We shall see.

*Hi bear, meet poke. \xf0\x9f\x98\x9c
**Our commentary refers to the quality of these cases as much as it does the quantity and it\xe2\x80\x99s pretty darn hard for anyone to argue that the quality is there these days given the nature of these filings.
***One stat we would\xe2\x80\x99ve liked to see in Mr. Eisenband\xe2\x80\x99s recap: how many rounds of golf did he play in 1H\xe2\x80\x9924 versus 1H\xe2\x80\x9923. Inquiring minds want to know. We reckon that, too, is an awfully tough comp. \xf0\x9f\x98\x89
****There\xe2\x80\x99s been a lot of concern about the consumer the last week or so. We thought this was an interesting recap of management commentary on the subject:

Also, the high yield market doesn\xe2\x80\x99t appear too concerned:

\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8fAnnouncement\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8f

We\xe2\x80\x99ll be going \xe2\x80\x9coff-schedule\xe2\x80\x9d for the rest of the summer for a much-needed refresh. Be safe out there, y\xe2\x80\x99all.

\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8fUpdate: The Container Store Group Inc. ($TCS)\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8f

Since we initiated coverage on The Container Store Group Inc. ($TCS) at the beginning of the year\xe2\x80\xa6

\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5Leaky Container\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

Jan 28
Read full story

\xe2\x80\xa6 the stock has since fallen ~46% on the back of an S&P Global Ratings downgrade and two consecutive quarters of continued sales declines.

So let\xe2\x80\x99s rewind and take a look at some of the headline numbers for the past two quarters.

For Q4\xe2\x80\x9923, the company reported $206mm in consolidated net sales (a decrease of 20.7% YoY), a 21.8% comparable store sales decline, and a net loss per share of $1.24/share ($0.04/share loss on an adjusted basis).

That put FY\xe2\x80\x9923 net sales at $847.8 million (down 19.0% YoY), with same store sales down 19.7%, and net loss per share of $2.09/share ($0.31/share loss on an adjusted basis).

For Q1\xe2\x80\x9924, the company reported net sales of $181.9 million (down 12.2% YoY), and a 13.7% same store sales decline.

See below for gross and EBITDA margins:

But what\xe2\x80\x99s more interesting than the same store sales declines (8 straight quarters btw), and compressing EBITDA margins, is management\xe2\x80\x99s initiation of a \xe2\x80\x9cstrategic review\xe2\x80\x9d led by J.P. Morgan Securities LLC (\xe2\x80\x9cJPM\xe2\x80\x9d) and Latham Watkins LLP.*

Here\xe2\x80\x99s CEO, Satish Malhotra, on the Q4\xe2\x80\x9923 earnings call:

\xe2\x80\x9cThe company's board of directors, along with the management team, do not believe the company's current market value is reflective of its intrinsic value and are committed to acting in the best interest of the company and its stockholders. The company is being advised by J.P. Morgan and Latham & Watkins LLP. As the board evaluates potential strategic alternatives, we are suspending financial guidance.\xe2\x80\x9d

What does Mr. Malhotra hope to achieve with these \xe2\x80\x9cstrategic alternatives\xe2\x80\x9d? Here\xe2\x80\x99s some further comments from Mr. Malhotra on the Q1\xe2\x80\x9924 earnings call:

\xe2\x80\x9cThe Board and management continue to work on plans to refinance our credit facility and concurrently are continuing to review strategic alternatives in an effort to ensure we are maximizing both the potential of the business and returns for shareholders.\xe2\x80\x9d

Of course it\xe2\x80\x99s a refinancing. Here\xe2\x80\x99s the credit facility in question:

First check out those applicable margins on the interest rate. Yes, the company entered into the credit agreement during better times in \xe2\x80\x9812 \xf0\x9f\x98\xae\xe2\x80\x8d\xf0\x9f\x92\xa8. Next, take a look at those maturities: we\xe2\x80\x99ve got a wall coming in little more than a year.**

So to avoid any last minute liquidity crisis, the company thinks it\xe2\x80\x99s time to go to market.

Unfortunately, the abysmal FCF numbers \xe2\x80\xa6

\xe2\x80\xa6 are definitely not helping the process. The term loan is pricing in the low-to-mid 60s.

*The advisors have already racked up $1.6mm worth of fees in Q1\xe2\x80\x9924, we\xe2\x80\x99re sure there\xe2\x80\x99s plenty more to come.
**Notably, the revolver also contains a 1:1 fixed-charge coverage ratio covenant.

\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8fUpdate: Fisker Inc.\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8f

Someone call Zack Morris, because this one\xe2\x80\x99s getting saved by the bell. The Fisker Inc. cases look like they\xe2\x80\x99ll live to see another day in chapter 11!

We previously mentioned \xe2\x80\xa6

\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5Big Lots. Fisker. BUCA.\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

Aug 14
Read full story

\xe2\x80\xa6 that due to a fifth cash collateral motion, the Fisker debtors, Heights Capital Management (\xe2\x80\x9cHeights\xe2\x80\x9d) and the official committee of unsecured creditors (\xe2\x80\x9cUCC\xe2\x80\x9d) had until August 19, 2024 to reach a settlement and avoid a conversion to chapter 7. Again, we quote Heights\xe2\x80\x99 counsel, White & Case LLP\xe2\x80\x99s Scott Greissman:

\xe2\x80\x9cThis is probably the most telegraphed Chapter 7 conversion in history.\xe2\x80\x9d

And yet somehow, we\xe2\x80\x99re still here, alive and kicking \xe2\x80\xa6 kind of.

The parties saved these on the literal last day (August 19th) with a SIXTH (and hopefully last) cash collateral motion filed in conjunction with a term sheet of a proposed settlement. This proposed settlement will take the debtors through a chapter 11 plan of liquidation, avoiding everything professionals fear most \xe2\x80\x94 having their fees relegated to administrative claims, \xf0\x9f\x98\x9c.

You\xe2\x80\x99ll recall that this thing was teetering on a knife\xe2\x80\x99s edge when, shortly after its appointment, the UCC went on the warpath against Heights, suggesting that Heights\xe2\x80\x99 $183mm prepetition secured status was complete fubar. Meanwhile Heights was like:

So Heights, sensing a very costly litigation process (on top of an already tight liquidity situation), decided to push to convert the cases to chapter 7.*

But what\xe2\x80\x99s the best way to avoid expensive disputes? Settlement! So here we are. And per the proposed settlement terms, Heights will have its full amount of prepetition secured claims recognized in exchange for some juicy concessions given to GUCs. These concessions include a share of (i) the proceeds of IP/Austria Assets, (ii) cash as of the effective date and proceeds of non-IP assets, and (iii) proceeds from the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 litigation claims and causes of action.**

We would note that this is all still a little up in the air: there\xe2\x80\x99s a hearing later today, Wednesday, August 21 at 3pm ET to get the motions through. Fingers crossed guys!\xf0\x9f\xa4\x9e

*You can refer to our previous coverage here and here for a more comprehensive breakdown.
**Here\xe2\x80\x99s a full breakdown of the proposed allocation:
Further, as a reminder, the debtors have ~480 vehicles tied up in the Austria insolvency proceedings which were last quoted to be ~$10mm in gross proceeds. The settlement seems to be tied to a resolution on the Austrian proceedings.

\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8fUpdate: Enviva Inc.\xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8f

We\xe2\x80\x99re back with what we hope \xe2\x80\x94 \xf0\x9f\x99\x8f please please \xf0\x9f\x99\x8f \xe2\x80\x94 is our last update in Enviva Inc\xe2\x80\x99s chapter 11 bankruptcy cases that revolves around professional retentions. It\xe2\x80\x99s time to move on to the substance y\xe2\x80\x99all and see what the reorganization actually looks like! But first, yes, we\xe2\x80\x99re back with your seemingly regularly scheduled US Trustee (\xe2\x80\x9cUST\xe2\x80\x9d) versus debtors\xe2\x80\x99 counsel drama.

As a quick refresher, Vinson had previous business relations with a certain major equity holder in Enviva, Riverstone Holdings LLC (\xe2\x80\x9cRiverstone\xe2\x80\x9d), and the UST was like:

Judge Kenney agreed and denied Vinson\xe2\x80\x99s employment application. You can read about that initial decision in our coverage here:

\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5Viva Enviva Drama! Viva NJ!!\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

Jun 2
Read full story

But Vinson came back with a fury and filed a motion to reconsider, proposing to implement an ethical wall to resolve the issues. Annnnnd that also failed. You can read our coverage on that second decision here:

\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5Rite-Aid Lives to See Another Day.\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

Jul 3
Read full story

If you\xe2\x80\x99re keeping count, that\xe2\x80\x99s UST 2 - Vinson 0.

BUT Judge Kenney left one door open: Vinson could come back in the future as special counsel under 327(e), stipulating that:

\xe2\x80\x9cThe Court anticipates that V&E would respect the limits of any employment under Section 327(e), and that V&E would not duplicate efforts by Section 327(a) counsel.\xe2\x80\x9d

And so after the debtors turned to Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison LLP (\xe2\x80\x9cPaul Weiss\xe2\x80\x9d) as new counsel, Vinson slapped an application to serve as special counsel onto the docket. In response to which \xe2\x80\xa6 LOLOLOLOL, the UST slapped an objection, LOLOLOLOLOLOLOL:

At this point Vinson be like:

The UST argued that Vinson\xe2\x80\x99s proposed services as special counsel were outside the scope of bankruptcy code section 327(e) and wayyyy too broad (and vague). Specifically, the UST expressed concerns over Vinson providing advice related to the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 DIP financing and contract negotiations (PETITION Note: this entire case is predicated upon financing and contract issues, lol).

Luckily, the parties were able to strike a rare compromise and arrive at a revised proposed order and \xe2\x80\x94 \xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8fthank the good Lord \xe2\x9a\xa1\xef\xb8\x8f\xe2\x80\x94 Judge Kenney approved Vinson\xe2\x80\x99s application on August 15, 2024.

And so the saga ends with a score line of UST 2.5 to Vinson 0.5. Will the UST just take the win or will we see another objection pop up with respect to, say, a future Vinson fee app? We\xe2\x80\x99ll be on the lookout.

In the meantime, Paul Weiss has some contracts to deal with!

Thank you for reading PETITION. This post is public so feel free to share it.

\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5New Chapter 11 Bankruptcy - AIO US Inc.\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

There\xe2\x80\x99s been such a surprise uptick in chapter 11 bankruptcy activity this month that we simply ran out of room on Wednesday to cover this one\xe2\x80\xa6.

On August 13, 2024, New York-based AIO US Inc. and three affiliates (collectively, the \xe2\x80\x9cdebtors\xe2\x80\x9d) filed chapter 11 bankruptcy cases in the District of Delaware (Judge Goldblatt) \xe2\x80\x94 the latest example of a large corporate using the bankruptcy process to manage mass tort liability. One of the debtor affiliates is Avon Products Inc. (\xe2\x80\x9cAPI\xe2\x80\x9d), the non-operational holding company of the legendary beauty brand\xe2\x80\x99s non-debtor, non-U.S., but entirely healthy entities. The debtors, facing considerable talc litigation liabilities and expenses, have effectively partnered with Natura SA (\xe2\x80\x9cNatura\xe2\x80\x9d), a Brazilian cosmetics giant with vast pre-petition ties to the debtors, to contend with nearly 400 separate lawsuits related to asbestos in talc products.

Naturally, the debtors are of the view that the lawsuits are all nonsense. \xe2\x80\x9cThe Debtors strongly believe this litigation is without merit,\xe2\x80\x9d CRO Phil Gund of Ankura Consulting Group states. The debtors plan to counter all plaintiffs with a \xe2\x80\x9cvigorous defense.\xe2\x80\x9d Ring those cash registers for the lawyers, y\xe2\x80\x99all!

So far, the \xe2\x80\x9cvigorous defense\xe2\x80\x9d has devoured some $225mm of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 funds, between legal bills and settlements. Gund runs through the parade of horribles: a Dec. \xe2\x80\x9822 California jury award to plaintiffs of $36mm in compensatory and $10mm damages (subsequently reduced to $29mm in compensatory damages but now subject to appeal); and separately, a Illinois jury award of $24.5mm to certain plaintiffs. The debtors plan to appeal, believing they have strong grounds to overturn the verdict. The upshot? As of the petition date, the debtors have some $78.1mm of unsecured talc liabilities.

Of course that\xe2\x80\x99s not the extent of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 financial story. Indeed, in total, there\xe2\x80\x99s $1.29b of funded debt obligations, including over $800mm in secured debt, nearly all of which is owed to various companies affiliated with Natura.

Which gets us to the parties around the table. Clearly the debt and litigation liabilities are getting to be too much so it was time for talks. In its corner, Natura hired Davis Polk & Wardell LLP (Darren Klein, Elliot Moskowitz, Hailey Klabo, Joshua Sturm) as legal counsel, FTI Consulting Inc. ($FCN) as financial advisor and PJT Partners LP ($PJT) as investment bank. For their part, the debtors brought on Weil Gotshal & Manges LLP (Ronit Berkovich, Matthew Goren, Alejandro Bascoy, Jessical Falk, Wesley Sheker, Carlos Sandina, Jiaheng Zhang) and Richards Layton & Finger PA (Mark Collins, Michael Merchant, Zachary Shapiro, David Queroli) as legal counsel; Ankura Consulting Group LLC (Mr Gund, who you know) as financial advisor and CRO, and Rothschild & Co. (\xe2\x80\x9cRothschild\xe2\x80\x9d)(Andrew Yearley, Marcello Marcin, Pratyush Hiremath) as investment banker. The company also brought on an independent, John Dubel, a regular in Weil\xe2\x80\x99s stable, onto the board to serve as a one-man \xe2\x80\x9cspecial committee.\xe2\x80\x9d

The plan (small p)? Let Natura deal with this mess. Gund says the debtors didn\xe2\x80\x99t have sufficient time to market their assets before filing, given liquidity constraints. Therefore, \xe2\x80\x9cin light of Natura\xe2\x80\x99s substantial amount of prepetition debt secured by liens in the Debtors\xe2\x80\x99 assets,\xe2\x80\x9d and considering Natura\xe2\x80\x99s in-depth knowledge of selling blush and foundation door to door, \xe2\x80\x9cthe Debtors determined Natura was the most logical to serve as a stalking horse bidder for the sale.\xe2\x80\x9d

The debtors\xe2\x80\x99 bid procedures motion hit the docket on August 14, 2024. The upshot is that Natura agreed to serve as stalking horse purchaser, with a credit bid of $125mm. There is no break up fee or expense reimbursement. To be ever-more accommodating, Natura has also agreed to act as a back-up bidder in the event that someone comes in with a higher or better offer. Natura has also agreed to assume a huge swatch of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 liabilities subject to exclusions delineated in the proposed stalking horse agreement.

Pretty sweet! So, how are we gonna pay for all this? Rothschild did solicit DIP financing from other parties, \xe2\x80\x9cincluding bulge bracket banks and asset management funds\xe2\x80\x9d but none \xe2\x80\x9cwere willing to lend to the debtors on a junior basis.\xe2\x80\x9d Natura returns to save the day: it has offered a $43mm superpriority senior secured DIP facility with an initial draw of $12mm. And here\xe2\x80\x99s something from Gund\xe2\x80\x99s declaration you don\xe2\x80\x99t see much these days:

The proposed DIP Financing is on terms that are very favorable to the estate\xe2\x80\x94of particular note, the proposed DIP Financing does not contemplate a \xe2\x80\x9croll-up\xe2\x80\x9d of any of Natura\xe2\x80\x99s prepetition secured loans. The proposed DIP Financing also does not include any unused commitment, exit, or agent fee and permits the Debtors to pay below-market interest in kind during the chapter 11 cases. Additionally, the DIP Lender is not seeking payment from the Debtors of any professional fees or expenses incurred in connection with the ordinary course negotiation or approval of the DIP Facility.

Hopefully that\xe2\x80\x99s not news to Davis Polk, FTI and PJT, lol.

Anyway, remember the aforementioned reference to Mr. Dubel? Was that just a pointless name drop on our part? NO! He\xe2\x80\x99s had work to do. He investigated \xe2\x80\x9ccertain claims of causes of action\xe2\x80\x9d and potential avoidance actions arising from the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 pre-petition transactions with Natura. More specifically, \xe2\x80\x9c(i) constructive fraudulent transfer, (ii) breach of fiduciary duty, (iii) piercing the corporate veil, and (iv) avoidance actions under the Bankruptcy Code.\xe2\x80\x9d He tapped Weil for help. Weil did the interviews, public document reviews, and engaged with Ankura and other advisors.

And guess what!

They found some stuff. We\xe2\x80\x99ll spare you the details but there was enough \xe2\x80\x9cthere there\xe2\x80\x9d in both the preference claims and constructive fraudulent transfer realms to force everyone back to the table for a good ol\xe2\x80\x99-fashioned arms-length chat.

And? There\xe2\x80\x99s a settlement. We\xe2\x80\x99ll spare you all the gritty details here too but suffice it to say that Natura is playing ball. It has agreed to, among other things, (i) waive some $530mm of secured debt principal, (ii) provide $30mm in cash to fund admin and wind-down costs and distributions to creditors, (iii) leave behind other assets that could enhance creditor recoveries (e.g., additional cash, an insurance receivable, certain preference claims), and (iv) assume a defined benefit pension plan. A hearing to approve the settlement is currently scheduled for October 15, 2024. In the interim, Rothschild will be busy trying to find a competing bidder; it has 67 days from the petition date to do so.

So for those of you keeping track at home, Natura is acting as the pre-petition lender, the proposed DIP lender, the stalking horse purchaser (and potential back-up bidder), AND the settlement counterparty. We\xe2\x80\x99re sure the UST will have nothing to say about THAT.

\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5New Chapter 11 Bankruptcy - Barrow Shaver Resources Company LLC\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5

On August 19, 2024, Barrow Shaver Resources Co. LLC (\xe2\x80\x9cBSR\xe2\x80\x9d or the \xe2\x80\x9cdebtor\xe2\x80\x9d), a Texas-based oil and gas exploration (\xe2\x80\x9cE&P\xe2\x80\x9d) company, filed a chapter 11 bankruptcy case in the Southern District of Texas (Judge Perez). The debtor was founded by two wildcatters, the last of a dying breed of oilmen willing to risk everything on unproven acreage. Big rewards if right. If not? Well \xe2\x80\xa6 \xe2\x80\x9cBetween 2023 and 2024, the Debtor conducted an aggressive drilling program in the East Texas Basin. The drilling program resulted in significant capex expenditures that far exceeded the Debtor\xe2\x80\x99s corresponding production gains,\xe2\x80\x9d CRO James Katchadurian, a partner at CR3 Partners LLC writes in his first day declaration. BSR could not cover its portion of the tab. In July, a group of creditors \xe2\x80\x94 oilfield service companies, the ones who do the dirty work \xe2\x80\x94 filed an involuntary petition under chapter 7 on the basis of $8.45mm in outstanding trade claims.

That\xe2\x80\x99s just the tip of the iceberg. While there is no funded secured debt on the debtor\xe2\x80\x99s balance sheet as of the petition date, it did owe a not-insignificant $67mm to its vendors, service providers and suppliers. Several working interest owners did not participate in the drilling program, leaving the debtors to shoulder more of the cost. The debtor also received some $40mm in lien perfection notices. Further complicating matters is a separate and complex issue related to mineral interests and tax payments, in connection with which midstream giant Plains Marketing LLP withheld $2.5mm owed the debtor and filed a complaint. The debtor has submitted a proposed order addressing, in part, the mineral interests situation which, given the adversarial kick-off to this case, understandably drew the ire of the involuntary petitioners. As if that\xe2\x80\x99s not enough drama, there\xe2\x80\x99s also a separate matter about working interest assignment in other East Texas wells, and district court cases concerning leases. This is CERTAINLY not one of those \xe2\x80\x9cquality\xe2\x80\x9d cases we referred to above.

The debtor seeks DIP financing so that it may undertake an asset sale and confirm a plan of reorganization that will provide \xe2\x80\x9ca significant distribution to creditors on account of their allowed claims.\xe2\x80\x9d (A presentation at the first day hearing indicated liquidation was also possible.) According to Katchadurian, the debtor has obtained a \xe2\x80\x9cfirm commitment\xe2\x80\x9d for DIP financing from \xe2\x80\x9cone interested party.\xe2\x80\x9d

Besides CR3 Partners as financial advisor, the debtor is represented by Jones Walker LLP (Joseph E. Bain, Mark A. Mintz, Sean T. Wilson, Olivia K. Greenberg, Elizabeth De Leon).

he debtors, facing considerable talc litigation liabilities and expenses, have effectively partnered with Natura SA (\xe2\x80\x9cNatura\xe2\x80\x9d), a Brazilian cosmetics giant with vast pre- petition ties to the debtors, to contend with nearly 400 separate lawsuits related to asbestos in talc products. Naturally, the debtors are of the view that the lawsuits are all nonsense. \xe2\x80\x9cThe Debtors strongly believe this litigation is without merit,\xe2\x80\x9d CRO Phil Gund of Ankura Consulting Group states. The debtors plan to counter all plaintiffs with a \xe2\x80\x9cvigorous defense.\xe2\x80\x9d Ring those cash registers for the lawyers, y\xe2\x80\x99all! So far, the \xe2\x80\x9c vigorous defense \xe2\x80\x9d has devoured some $225mm of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 funds, between legal bills and settlements. Gund runs through the parade of horribles: a Dec. \xe2\x80\x9822 California jury award to plaintiffs of $36mm in compensatory and $10mm damages (subsequently reduced to $29mm in compensatory damages but now subject to appeal); and separately, a Illinois jury award of $24.5mm to certain plaintiffs. The debtors plan to appeal, believing they have strong grounds to overturn the verdict. The upshot? As of the petition date, the debtors have some $78.1mm of unsecured talc liabilities. Of course that\xe2\x80\x99s not the extent of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 financial story. Indeed, in total, there\xe2\x80\x99s $1.29b of funded debt obligations, including over $800mm in secured debt, nearly all of which is owed to various companies affiliated with Natura. Which gets us to the parties around the table. Clearly the debt and litigation liabilities are getting to be too much so it was time for talks. In its corner, Natura hired Davis Polk & Wardell LLP (Darren Klein, Elliot Moskowitz, Hailey Klabo, Joshua Sturm) as legal counsel, FTI Consulting Inc. ($FCN) as financial advisor and PJT Partners LP ($PJT) as investment bank. For their part, the debtors brought on Weil Gotshal & Manges LLP (Ronit Berkovich, Matthew Goren, Alejandro Bascoy, Jessical Falk, Wesley Sheker, Carlos Sandina, Jiaheng Zhang) and Richards Layton & Finger PA (Mark Collins, Michael Merchant, Zachary Shapiro, David Queroli) as legal counsel; Ankura Consulting Group LLC (Mr Gund, who you know) as financial advisor and CRO, and Rothschild & Co. (\xe2\x80\x9cRothschild\xe2\x80\x9d)(Andrew Yearley, Marcello Marcin, Pratyush Hiremath) as investment banker. The company also brought on an independent, John Dubel , a regular in Weil\xe2\x80\x99s stable, onto the board to serve as a one- man \xe2\x80\x9cspecial committee.\xe2\x80\x9d The plan (small p)? Let Natura deal with this mess. Gund says the debtors didn\xe2\x80\x99t have sufficient time to market their assets before filing, given liquidity constraints. Therefore, \xe2\x80\x9c in light of Natura\xe2\x80\x99s substantial amount of prepetition debt secured by liens in the Debtors\xe2\x80\x99 assets,\xe2\x80\x9d and considering Natura\xe2\x80\x99s in- depth knowledge of selling blush and foundation door to door , \xe2\x80\x9cthe Debtors determined Natura was the most logical to serve as a stalking horse bidder for the sale.\xe2\x80\x9d The debtors\xe2\x80\x99 bid procedures motion hit the docket on August 14, 2024. The upshot is that Natura agreed to serve as stalking horse purchaser, with a credit bid of $125mm. There is no break up fee or expense reimbursement. To be ever- more accommodating, Natura has also agreed to act as a back- up bidder in the event that someone comes in with a higher or better offer. Natura has also agreed to assume a huge swatch of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 liabilities subject to exclusions delineated in the proposed stalking horse agreement. Pretty sweet! So, how are we gonna pay for all this? Rothschild did solicit DIP financing from other parties, \xe2\x80\x9cincluding bulge bracket banks and asset management funds\xe2\x80\x9d but none \xe2\x80\x9cwere willing to lend to the debtors on a junior basis.\xe2\x80\x9d Natura returns to save the day: it has offered a $43mm superpriority senior secured DIP facility with an initial draw of $12mm. And here\xe2\x80\x99s something from Gund\xe2\x80\x99s declaration you don\xe2\x80\x99t see much these days: The proposed DIP Financing is on terms that are very favorable to the estate\xe2\x80\x94 of particular note, the proposed DIP Financing does not contemplate a \xe2\x80\x9croll- up\xe2\x80\x9d of any of Natura\xe2\x80\x99s prepetition secured loans. The proposed DIP Financing also does not include any unused commitment, exit, or agent fee and permits the Debtors to pay below- market interest in kind during the chapter 11 cases. Additionally, the DIP Lender is not seeking payment from the Debtors of any professional fees or expenses incurred in connection with the ordinary course negotiation or approval of the DIP Facility. Hopefully that\xe2\x80\x99s not news to Davis Polk, FTI and PJT, lol. Anyway, remember the aforementioned reference to Mr. Dubel? Was that just a pointless name drop on our part? NO! He\xe2\x80\x99s had work to do. He investigated \xe2\x80\x9ccertain claims of causes of action\xe2\x80\x9d and potential avoidance actions arising from the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 pre- petition transactions with Natura. More specifically, \xe2\x80\x9c (i) constructive fraudulent transfer, (ii) breach of fiduciary duty, (iii) piercing the corporate veil, and (iv) avoidance actions under the Bankruptcy Code.\xe2\x80\x9d He tapped Weil for help. Weil did the interviews, public document reviews, and engaged with Ankura and other advisors. And guess what! They found some stuff. We\xe2\x80\x99ll spare you the details but there was enough \xe2\x80\x9cthere there\xe2\x80\x9d in both the preference claims and constructive fraudulent transfer realms to force everyone back to the table for a good ol\xe2\x80\x99- fashioned arms- length chat. And? There\xe2\x80\x99s a settlement. We\xe2\x80\x99ll spare you all the gritty details here too but suffice it to say that Natura is playing ball. It has agreed to, among other things, (i) waive some $530mm of secured debt principal, (ii) provide $30mm in cash to fund admin and wind- down costs and distributions to creditors, (iii) leave behind other assets that could enhance creditor recoveries (e.g., additional cash, an insurance receivable, certain preference claims), and (iv) assume a defined benefit pension plan. A hearing to approve the settlement is currently scheduled for October 15, 2024. In the interim, Rothschild will be busy trying to find a competing bidder; it has 67 days from the petition date to do so. So for those of you keeping track at home, Natura is acting as the pre- petition lender, the proposed DIP lender, the stalking horse purchaser (and potential back- up bidder), AND the settlement counterparty. We\xe2\x80\x99re sure the UST will have nothing to say about THAT. \xf0\x9f\x92\xa5New Chapter 11 Bankruptcy Barrow Shaver Resources Company LLC\xf0\x9f\x92\xa5 On August 19, 2024, Barrow Shaver Resources Co. LLC (\xe2\x80\x9cBSR\xe2\x80\x9d or the \xe2\x80\x9cdebtor\xe2\x80\x9d), a Texas- based oil and gas exploration (\xe2\x80\x9cE&P\xe2\x80\x9d) company, filed a chapter 11 bankruptcy case in the Southern District of Texas ( Judge Perez ). The debtor was founded by two wildcatters, the last of a dying breed of oilmen willing to risk everything on unproven acreage. Big rewards if right. If not? Well \xe2\x80\xa6 \xe2\x80\x9c Between 2023 and 2024, the Debtor conducted an aggressive drilling program in the East Texas Basin. The drilling program resulted in significant capex expenditures that far exceeded the Debtor\xe2\x80\x99s corresponding production gains,\xe2\x80\x9d CRO James Katchadurian , a partner at CR3 Partners LLC writes in his first day declaration . BSR could not cover its portion of the tab. In July, a group of creditors oilfield service companies, the ones who do the dirty work filed an involuntary petition under chapter 7 on the basis of $8.45mm in outstanding trade claims. That\xe2\x80\x99s just the tip of the iceberg. While there is no funded secured debt on the debtor\xe2\x80\x99s balance sheet as of the petition date, it did owe a not- insignificant $67mm to its vendors, service providers and suppliers. Several working interest owners did not participate in the drilling program, leaving the debtors to shoulder more of the cost. The debtor also received some $40mm in lien perfection notices. Further complicating matters is a separate and complex issue related to mineral interests and tax payments, in connection with which midstream giant Plains Marketing LLP withheld $2.5mm owed the debtor and filed a complaint. The debtor has submitted a proposed order addressing, in part, the mineral interests situation which, given the adversarial kick- off to this case, understandably drew the ire of the involuntary petitioners. As if that\xe2\x80\x99s not enough drama, there\xe2\x80\x99s also a separate matter about working interest assignment in other East Texas wells, and district court cases concerning leases. This is CERTAINLY not one of those \xe2\x80\x9cquality\xe2\x80\x9d cases we referred to above. The debtor seeks DIP financing so that it may undertake an asset sale and confirm a plan of reorganization that will provide \xe2\x80\x9ca significant distribution to creditors on account of their allowed claims.\xe2\x80\x9d (A presentation at the first day hearing indicated liquidation was also possible.) According to Katchadurian , the debtor has obtained a \xe2\x80\x9cfirm commitment\xe2\x80\x9d for DIP financing from \xe2\x80\x9cone interested party.\xe2\x80\x9d Besides CR3 Partners as financial advisor, the debtor is represented by Jones Walker LLP (Joseph E. Bain, Mark A. Mintz, Sean T. Wilson, Olivia K. Greenberg, Elizabeth De Leon). \xf0\x9f\x93\x9aResources\xf0\x9f\x93\x9a We have compiled a list of a$$- kicking resources on the topics of restructuring, tech, finance, investing, and disruption. \xf0\x9f\x92\xa5 You can find it here \xf0\x9f\x92\xa5. Of course that\xe2\x80\x99s not the extent of the debtors\xe2\x80\x99 financial story. Indeed, in total, there\xe2\x80\x99s $1.29b of funded debt obligations, including over $800mm in secured debt, nearly all of which is owed to various companies affiliated with Natura. Which gets us to the parties around the table. Clearly the debt and litigation liabilities are getting to be too much so it was time for talks. In its corner, Natura hired Davis Polk & Wardell LLP (Darren Klein, Elliot Moskowitz, Hailey Klabo, Joshua Sturm) as legal counsel, FTI Consulting Inc. ($FCN) as financial advisor and PJT Partners LP ($PJT) as investment bank. For their part, the debtors brought on Weil Gotshal & Manges LLP (Ronit Berkovich, Matthew Goren, Alejandro Bascoy, Jessical Falk, Wesley Sheker, Carlos Sandina, Jiaheng Zhang) and Richards Layton & Finger PA (Mark Collins, Michael Merchant, Zachary Shapiro, David Queroli) as legal counsel; Ankura Consulting Group LLC (Mr Gund, who you know) as financial advisor and CRO, and Rothschild & Co.
